mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 50%) when tested over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index during a deep recession signals stabilizing manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term equity rallies over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes during recessionary periods do not yield statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI decline exceeding 0.3 percentage points confirms disinflationary pressure that removes constraints on central bank easing, prompting positive equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain negative or flat following CPI declines above the threshold, with a cumulative 20-day return below -1% in over 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates confirm structural labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate income and forces sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces a positive 60-day return in more than 50% of triggered observations, invalidating the negative earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly contractions in real GDP directly reduce corporate revenue expectations and dividend payout capacity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth rates fail to predict negative 60-day equity returns, with a rank correlation coefficient consistently below 0.05 across all sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Stabilization in industrial production coinciding with a federal funds rate cut signals that monetary accommodation is successfully offsetting supply-side weakness, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of stabilizing INDPRO and falling FEDFUNDS yields negative 20-day returns in over 55% of instances, indicating policy transmission failure.
